snapshotBar.fixedTip: Created via the wizard — the configuration was locked in at creation and runs with the task. To change it, start a new task from the launchpad.

wizard.dropHint: Drag video or subtitle files here, or use the button in the top-right
wizard.pairHint: Add videos together with subtitles to pair them by name automatically (skips transcription); pick manually on a video row when names differ
wizard.pairTxtUnsupported: txt subtitles have no timeline and cannot be paired

goal.subtitle: Subtitles
goal.translateDesc: Translate subtitles into a target language
goal.dubDesc: Read the subtitles with an AI voice
goal.videoDesc: Compose subtitles (and dubbing) into the video

wizard.goalSubtitlePaired: Uses your matching subtitles — transcription is skipped
wizard.chainEmpty: Pick goals above to preview the workflow
wizard.dubBillingHint: Cloud dubbing bills by characters; cost depends on the final subtitle text
wizard.composeAudioHint: The dub track will replace the original audio automatically
wizard.composeStyleHint: Fine-tune styles in the compose workbench and save them as my styles.
wizard.gateSubtitleDesc: Pause after subtitles for your check before dubbing/composing
wizard.gateDubbingDesc: Pause after dubbing for a listen before composing
wizard.gatesHint: You'll be notified at each stop; release to continue automatically. Turn both off for fully automatic.
wizard.blockNoFiles: Add the files to process first
wizard.blockVideoNeedsMedia: Final video needs video input — uncheck it or add video files
wizard.blockUnpairedMedia: {{count}} video(s) have no subtitle paired — pick one on the video row, add files, or remove them
wizard.readyHint: Ready: {{count}} file(s) will be processed automatically

recipe.dialogDesc: Save the current goals and settings as a recipe — reuse it from the Launchpad with one click or a file drop.
recipe.namePlaceholder: Recipe name, e.g. English video → Chinese dubbed film
recipe.saved: Recipe "{{name}}" saved — find it on the Launchpad

newTaskHint: New task · saved to recent tasks once files are added
advancedDesc: Details you rarely change — the defaults are best practice

accurate.title: Most accurate text
accurate.desc: Finest segmentation and the most complete punctuation; keeps each sentence as faithfully as possible. May occasionally add a stray line over silence/background noise.
accurate.scene: Best for lectures, interviews, talking-head videos where complete text matters most

balanced.title: Balanced
balanced.desc: Complete yet clean text with a stable timeline; stray lines over silence are basically gone — a safe choice for most videos.
balanced.scene: Best for most videos; pick this if unsure

clean.title: Cleanest & most stable
clean.desc: Suppresses repeats and hallucinated lines most aggressively with the most restrained text; may drop some unclear speech.
clean.scene: Best for long videos or material with music / applause / long silence

custom.title: Custom
custom.desc: Manually tune context length, VAD, repetition reduction and other low-level parameters.
custom.sherpaNote: For the current engine (FunASR / Qwen / FireRed), VAD is always on and there is no context-length / repetition concept. To fine-tune VAD sensitivity, use the Settings page.

compare.caption: Roughly how the same speech (with a short silence at the end) appears at each level:
compare.accurate: Today, let's go over this new feature. Thanks for watching.
compare.balanced: Today let's go over this new feature.
compare.clean: Today let's go over this new feature

vad.label: Voice activity detection (VAD)
vad.on: Faster and steadier: the built-in whisper engine only transcribes inside detected speech, skipping silence/background noise and greatly reducing repeated or hallucinated lines there; lines are aligned token-by-token to the speech segments, so the timeline hugs the speech and stays stable.
vad.off: Most complete text: the built-in whisper engine segments more finely with fuller punctuation (recommended for short/medium files and Chinese); stray lines over silence are curbed by an energy fallback; other engines such as faster-whisper may still produce repeated or hallucinated lines over silence/music.
vad.hint: Turn off for the most complete text and finest segmentation; keep on for the steadiest timeline and fewest stray lines over silence. This is a global setting and affects all tasks.

reduceRepetition.label: Reduce repetition / hallucinated lines
reduceRepetition.on: On: drops previous-text conditioning and suppresses repeats, greatly reducing looped lines over silence/repetition; coherence on clean speech may drop slightly.
reduceRepetition.off: Off: engine defaults (keeps cross-segment coherence); long videos or silence may produce repeated or hallucinated lines.
reduceRepetition.hint: Turn on when subtitles loop or repeat heavily. Global setting; applies to both faster-whisper and whisper.cpp.

subtitleLength.label: Subtitle line breaking
subtitleLength.hintSmart: The engine splits lines at pauses and punctuation, keeping each subtitle at a readable length.
subtitleLength.hintUnlimited: Split only at speech pauses and punctuation. Lines are never force-split by length and may run long.
subtitleLength.hintCustom: Lines over the limit are split at punctuation or word boundaries, never inside a word. CJK characters count as 2 (40 is about 20 Chinese characters); 30-50 suggested for Chinese, 40-60 for English.

chinesePunctuation.label: Strip Chinese punctuation
chinesePunctuation.hint: Replace Chinese punctuation (，。、？！ etc.) in Chinese subtitles with spaces and trim trailing spaces for cleaner subtitles. Applies to the source when the source language is Chinese, and to the translation when the target is Chinese.

gate.subtitle: Subtitle review
gate.reviewTooltip: This file is waiting for your review; it continues automatically once released
gate.releaseAllDesc: {{count}} file(s) will skip review and continue processing (dubbing/composing may take time and incur cost).

row.proofreadTxtUnsupported: TXT files do not include timing, so they cannot be proofread here. Generate SRT/VTT/ASS for proofreading, then export plain text after proofreading.

interrupted: Interrupted by app restart — retry to continue

completion.title: {{done}} files completed
completion.sampleTitle: Sample done — your first subtitle is ready!
completion.sampleHint: Open it in proofread, or head back to the launchpad to process your own files

empty.dragMedia: You can also drop media files or folders anywhere on this page
empty.dragSubtitle: You can also drop subtitle files or folders anywhere on this page
empty.mediaFormats: Supports MP4 / MKV / MOV / MP3 / WAV and 24+ more video & audio formats
empty.subtitleFormats: Supports SRT / VTT / ASS / SSA / LRC subtitles
empty.step2Desc: Pick engine, model and languages in the config bar above; advanced options are top right
empty.step3Desc: Hit Start in the bottom-right corner to batch-process with live progress
